meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn how to engineer SaaS pricing models that validate market demand using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no project idea' trap is actually a lack of pricing validation. Instead of brainstorming, build a pricing-first landing page. If users won't click a 'Pay' button, the idea is dead. Use Antigravity to scaffold the architecture and Claude Code to iterate your pricing logic rapidly against real market feedback.
content.intro:
The screen is blank, the cursor blinks like a heartbeat, and the 'no project ideas' paralysis sets in. You’ve been here before—chasing features instead of revenue, building software that nobody is actually incentivized to buy. Most founders treat pricing as an afterthought, a spreadsheet task for 'later.' This is the fatal flaw that kills 90% of SaaS startups before they reach their first MRR.

To break the cycle, we stop building products and start building offers. By integrating AI-driven scaffolding with rigorous pricing experiments, we shift from 'coding for fun' to 'engineering for value.' This isn't just about picking a number; it's about architecting a business model that forces market validation from day one.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, map out three distinct tiers based on user outcomes, not feature lists. We need to identify the 'Aha!' moment that triggers a willingness to pay.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS pricing strategist. Create a JSON schema for a tiered pricing model (Starter, Pro, Enterprise) based on usage-based metrics for a B2B productivity tool. Prioritize high-conversion psychological anchors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a pricing-table.tsx component using Tailwind CSS that highlights the middle tier as the primary conversion target.'

steps.title: Build the 'Fake Door' Validation Hook
steps.description: Deploy a pricing page that tracks intent. If users engage with your pricing tiers, you have a project idea worth pursuing. If they don't, pivot immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a landing page architecture using Next.js that includes a pricing component and a 'Buy Now' button that logs click-through rates to a Supabase table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add analytics tracking to the pricing button clicks and integrate with a mock payment gateway log.'

steps.title: Architect the Usage-Based Billing Logic
steps.description: Implement the backend logic that connects usage to pricing. This ensures your MVP is inherently tied to revenue generation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe webhook handler in Node.js that updates a user's subscription status based on usage thresholds defined in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Refactor the backend billing controller to handle tiered usage limits and trigger an alert when a user hits 80% of their quota.'

steps.title: Automate Pricing Iteration via A/B Testing
steps.description: Use AI to adjust pricing variables based on conversion data. This rapid cycle prevents the stagnation of 'no ideas' by keeping the product dynamic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a configuration system that allows for dynamic pricing updates without redeploying the entire application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement a feature flag system to toggle between two pricing models for A/B testing on the landing page.'

steps.title: Finalize the Revenue-First MVP
steps.description: Review the integrated system to ensure the path from signup to payment is frictionless and mathematically sound.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a security and revenue flow audit on the current pricing implementation, ensuring all payment hooks are correctly secured.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Run a complete end-to-end test of the signup-to-subscription flow and identify any potential bottlenecks in the conversion funnel.'

pro_tips: Always anchor your highest tier at 3x the price of your middle tier to make the middle option feel like a bargain.
Never build a free tier until you have 10 paying customers; free users provide zero validation signals.
Use usage-based billing from day one to align your product’s success with the customer’s success.
The 'No Idea' problem is solved by talking to customers, not by staring at an IDE. Use your pricing page as a survey tool.
If your pricing page doesn't convert at 2% within the first 48 hours, kill the idea and pivot to the next one instantly.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing page to Vercel, share it on niche communities like IndieHackers or X, and monitor the conversion data for 48 hours to validate your project idea.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn a blank screen into a revenue-generating asset. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building a validated business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
